body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
- Use a TimeVaryingInput for ground forcing, not a function. This unifies the ground forcing with how we handle atmos and radiative forcing
- Move the PrescribedGroundConditions, PrognosticGround, and PrognosticSoil to src/shared_utilities/drivers.jl. These are very analogous to prescribed/coupled atmosphere and radiation drivers (prescribed or prognostic), so we should follow the same convention
- time varying soil properties are now updated via callback rather than every step in standalone canopy runs
- move ground_albedo methods for integrated models to land_radiation.jl instead of individual files


## To-do


## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->


<!---

- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Refactor PrescribedGroundConditions; use time varying input

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
In #1233, we added convenience constructors for canopy component models. These constructors provide defaults for many parameters that can easily be overwritten.

However, we forgot to use `get_default_parameter` as the defaults for some parameters that are available via ClimaParams. Note that some parameters are still hardcoded because they aren't in ClimaParams, but eventually we'd like to add them there as well.
title: use get_default_parameter for canopy parameters
